description: The Tn10 divergent tetA/tetR control region: 56 bp carrying the PtetA promoter, the overlapping PtetR promoter, and the two TetR operators tetO1 and tetO2.

The Tn10-encoded tetR mRNA has heterogeneous 5' ends in vivo and in vitro

label: tetR/tetA promoters
note: The Tn10 divergent tetA/tetR control region: 56 bp carrying the PtetA promoter, the overlapping PtetR promoter, and the two TetR operators tetO1 and tetO2.

label: PtetA -35
note: Canonical sigma70 -35 hexamer (TTGACA) of the tetA promoter (PtetA), reading 5'->3' into tetA.

label: PtetA -10
note: Degenerate sigma70 -10 hexamer (TTATTT) of PtetA, positioned with a canonical 17 bp -35/-10 spacer (sequence[7:24] = CTCTATCATTGATAGAG).

label: tetO1
note: 19 bp near-dyad TetR operator O1 (CACTCTATCATTGATAGAG), superimposed on the PtetA -10/spacer.

label: tetO2
note: 19 bp non-palindromic TetR operator O2 (TCCCTATCAGTGATAGAGA), the higher-affinity wild-type operator, superimposed on the divergent PtetR -10 region; O2 main...

label: PtetA +1
note: tetA transcription start site (+1), ~7 nt downstream of the PtetA -10; the divergent tetA/tetR start sites were mapped by S1 analysis of tet mRNA 5' ends.

label: PtetR -35
note: Sigma-70 -35 hexamer of PtetR (bottom strand reads TTCTCT), the divergent promoter that transcribes tetR leftward; PtetR corresponds to the dominant tetR promoter (P_R2).

label: PtetR -10
note: Sigma-70 -10 hexamer of PtetR (bottom strand reads TAAAAT), ~18 bp downstream of the PtetR -35; it overlaps the PtetA -10 on the opposite strand in the divergent arrangement.

label: PtetR +1
note: tetR transcription start site (+1) of PtetR (P_R2), the promoter producing >95% of tetR mRNA in vivo; the start shows length heterogeneity from reiterative copying of a short run of A residues.

functional_claims.label: Divergent tetA/tetR control region: TetR-repressible / aTc-inducible.

source.quote: The Tn10 divergent tetA/tetR control region: 56 bp carrying the PtetA promoter, the overlapping PtetR promoter, and the two TetR operators tetO1 and tetO2.
